title: Zorba Desilijic Tiure
content:
{{Top|canon=Zorba}}
{{Update|''[[Secrets of Tatooine]]'', ''[[Who's Who in Jabba's Palace]]''}}
{{Character
|type=Hutt Cartel
|image=[[File:ZorbaMugshot-HNN.png]]
|name=Zorba Desilijic Tiure
|homeworld=[[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]]<ref name="EGTC">''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref>

|death=After [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]] {{C|rumored}}<ref name="HHH">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030911hutt|text=Hutt! Hutt! Hutt!}}</ref>

*[[Ebor/Legends|Ebor]]<ref name="HFZ" />
|children=[[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]]<ref name="HHH" />
|affiliation=*[[Desilijic/Legends|Desilijic]] [[Kajidic/Legends|kajidic]]<ref name="HHH" />

|masters=
|apprentices=

{{Quote|Zorba has been a shameful pus-filled boil on the glorious Hutt business community, and this sentencing is a heated lancing long overdue. His selfish antics have given an unwarranted criminal taint to the Hutt reputation that can now begin to rebuild with his absence.|Aruk Besadii Aora|<ref name="HNN1">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|48|regional/13321_5.html|Zorba the Hutt Sentenced to 45 Years}}</ref>}}
'''Zorba Desilijic Tiure''' was a [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] and the leader of the [[Desilijic/Legends|Desilijic]] [[Kajidic/Legends|kajidic]]. One of few Hutts to possess hair—as a result of a genetic throwback—Zorba was the brother of [[Jiliac Desilijic Tiron|Jiliac]], [[Ebor/Legends|Ebor]], [[Ziro Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Ziro]] and [[Pazda Desilijic Tiure|Pazda]], father of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]], and grandfather of [[Rotta/Legends|Rotta]], [[Gorga Desilijic Aarrpo/Legends|Gorga]] and [[Grubba Desilijic Aarrpo]]. Zorba was an inept leader of Desilijic, and he had few supporters in his clan due to his outlandish schemes and financial mismanagement. During the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], he was apprehended by law enforcement and sentenced to [[Prison/Legends|prison]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kip]]. Jiliac took over as Desilijic leader and Jabba inherited much of Zorba's holdings.

In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], Zorba was released by agents of [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]] [[Ysanne Isard]] and traveled to [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|Jabba's Palace]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] in his [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the ''[[Zorba Express]]''. Zorba was refused admission to the palace, and he later discovered that Jabba had been [[Battle of the Great Pit of Carkoon|killed]] by [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. Determined to punish Organa, Zorba put out a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on her. Using a [[Jabba the Hutt's Will|secret will]] of his son's, Zorba laid claim to much of Jabba's holdings throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] including his [[Holiday Towers Hotel and Casino|casino]] on [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]] and his desert citadel on Tatooine.

On Bespin, Zorba won control of [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] from [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and learned that Organa was in the [[City/Legends|city]], in [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Trioculus]]' captivity. He attempted to bargain with Trioculus for Organa to be handed over to him, but he refused, and Imperial forces clashed with Zorba's [[Bespin Wing Guard/Legends|Cloud Police]]. Zorba emerged on top, and believed he had killed Organa after destroying an Imperial factory barge. Zorba's minions later informed him that Organa had survived, but he captured her on [[Hologram Fun World]] and brought her to Tatooine, planning to feed her to the [[Sarlacc/Legends|sarlacc]]. Trioculus, however, rescued Organa, and fed Zorba himself to the sarlacc. Zorba escaped as well and helped Trioculus' rivals, the false [[Prophets of the Dark Side]], defeat him. Zorba regained leadership of the Desilijic clan, but Jabba's will was later declared a forgery by the [[Grand Hutt Council/Legends|Hutt Grand Council]]. Disgraced, Zorba went into hiding and was rumored to have died soon thereafter.

==Biography==
===Clan leader===
{{Quote|I have been very careful, over the years, not to compare you with your reckless parent, who nearly bankrupted Desilijic with his grand schemes…|Jiliac, speaking of Zorba, to Jabba|<ref name="Rebel Dawn">''[[Rebel Dawn]]''</ref>}}
Zorba Desilijic Tiure, a member of the [[Hutt/Legends|Huttese]] [[Desilijic/Legends|Desilijic]] clan, was born around [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]] on [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]]; he had three younger siblings: [[Jiliac Desilijic Tiron]], [[Pazda Desilijic Tiure]],<ref name="NEGTC" /> and the scheming [[Ziro Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Ziro Desilijic Tiure]].<ref name="cw" /> As a result of a recessive genetic trait, Zorba<ref name="HHH" /> (along with Pazda)<ref name="AoC">''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse]]''</ref> was one of few Hutts to possess any hair. He had long, white, dreadlocked hair on his head and a great, braided beard; because it was such a rarity in Hutts, Zorba's hair marked him as a kind of mutant among many of the other Hutts and they rarely hid their distaste at it. Zorba was also a powerful [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] and part of the Desilijic [[Kajidic/Legends|kajidic]], a type of Hutt criminal syndicate, one of the most powerful in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]; he, however, was an incompetent criminal leader.<ref name="HHH" />

[[File:Jabbarotj.jpg|thumb|right|Zorba's favorite child, Jabba Desilijic Tiure.]]By [[600 BBY/Legends|600 BBY]],<ref name="ascension">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' establishes that Jabba was born in 600 BBY, and ''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' establishes that Zorba was clan leader when Jabba was born.</ref> Zorba had ascended to the leadership of the Desilijic clan. The clan had previously been one of the most influential of the Hutt clans, but by the time of Zorba's life, it was falling on hard times and funds were low. An inept planner and organizer, Zorba's mismanagement of the clan made financial matters even worse;<ref name="HHH" /> ambitious and daring—to the point that some, such as his brother Jiliac, believed him completely reckless—Zorba attempted many grand schemes during his time as leader, each one a failure and each one further emptying Desilijic's coffer. However, although he was unpopular among many of his fellow clan members,<ref name="Rebel Dawn" /> Zorba ruled Desilijic with an iron fist and warded off any attempts at his ouster.<ref name="NEGTC" />

A [[Gender/Legends|male]] Hutt,<ref name="HHH" /> Zorba was also the [[Parent/Legends|father]] of multiple sons who took after their father.<ref name="DEE">[[Dark Empire endnotes|''Dark Empire'' endnotes]]</ref> One of them, [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]], was particularly criminally gifted,<ref name="HHH" /> and Zorba saw him as his only heir; he taught him all he knew: how to double-cross those who trusted him, how to deal with [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], how to deal with his enemies, and how to control [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]].<ref name="EGTC" /> Jabba thrived and became one of the youngest Hutts to maintain his own [[Jabba's criminal empire/Legends|criminal organization]]. Zorba himself was embroiled in a heated conflict with Desilijic's main rival clan, [[Besadii/Legends|Besadii]], and his opposite number, [[Aruk Besadii Aora]]; the two hated each other with a passion.<ref name="HNN1" /> At some point in his life, Zorba became a grandparent; his offspring produced two children of their own, [[Gorga Desilijic Aarrpo/Legends|Gorga Desilijic Aarrpo]] and [[Grubba Desilijic Aarrpo]],<ref name="NEGTC" /> and Jabba birthed a son, [[Rotta/Legends|Rotta]].<ref name="cw" />

===Imprisonment===
{{Quote|The tiny inhabitants of Kip have dealt a large blow to the Desilijic Hutt clan with the conviction of legitimate businessman Zorba Desilijic Tiure for the illegal removal of ulikuo stones.|[[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] report on Zorba's conviction|<ref name="HNN1" />}}
Despite Zorba's raising Jabba, he and his son were not close; Jabba kept to himself, running his own organization with efficiency Zorba was incapable of matching.<ref name="Rebel Dawn" /> Despite this, Zorba occasionally visited Jabba at his [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|desert palace]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], where he was always a welcome guest.<ref name="ZtHR" /> In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], Zorba was visiting his son when Jabba offered to free one of his slaves, [[Dorn (Bothan)|Dorn]], and allow him to work for Zorba. To prove his loyalty, however, Dorn was told to recapture the [[Ghostling]] children, whom he had previously freed from [[Gardulla Besadii the Elder/Legends|Gardulla the Hutt's]] custody. Dorn double-crossed Jabba, rescuing the children and escaping from Tatooine.<ref name="EpI">''[[Episode I Adventures 7: Capture Arawynne]]''</ref>

By this time, however, Zorba was extremely old; ancient even by Hutt standards, he was over 900 years old and one of the oldest Hutts in existence at that time. Zorba was also tired of being in charge of Desilijic, and seeing his plans end without fruition again and again. During the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], Zorba took his [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the ''[[Zorba Express]]'', to the backwater [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Kip]], hoping to [[Mining/Legends|mine]] valuable [[ulikuo gemstone]]s, much sought-after for their aesthetics; Zorba was unaware that mining them was illegal on Kip, and he was apprehended by local law enforcement,<ref name="HNN1" /> though he was able to hide his stash of gemstones.<ref name="ZtHR" /> Under pressure from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], who hoped to put Zorba behind bars for as long as possible, the government of Kip tried him in court and he was sentenced to forty-five standard years in the [[Girigard Subsurface Correctional Facility]], a far harsher sentence than what would normally have been given. Zorba's lawyer, [[Bequin Fobas]], attempted to appeal, but as Kip had no appeals process this was not possible; Zorba was held in prison, and the Republic sent additional resources preventing his escape.<ref name="HNN1" />

[[File:ZorbaExpress-ZorbaTheHuttsRevenge.jpg|thumb|left|220px|The ''Zorba Express''.]]
The Hutt clans were furious, and many believed that Zorba had been framed. Although he was unpopular, the [[Grand Hutt Council/Legends|Hutt Ruling Council]] attempted to extradite him to Nal Hutta to face charges there, but again the local laws on Kip prevented this from happening. Zorba's opponent, Aruk, addressed the public, saying that he was glad Zorba would not be able to taint Hutt society any longer; Zorba's few supporters desperately tried to prove that he had been set up by a rival, to no avail. Jiliac took over control of the Desilijic clan, and Jabba was quick to absorb much of Zorba's holdings on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]], [[Shaum Hii/Legends|Shaum Hii]] and [[Tyne's Horky]].<ref name="HNN1" /> Zorba's conviction had a negative effect on the financial affairs of Desilijic, and to recover from this, the clan had to cut funding to many [[Podracing/Legends|podracing]] circuits.<ref name="HHN2">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|sports/13328_1.html|Podracing Faces Uncertain Future}}</ref>

Zorba, however, was unperturbed by the whole affair. He knew that his properties and businesses in [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]] would be turned over to Jabba, who would do a far better job at managing them than Zorba had. Zorba knew that by Hutt law, Jabba would have to sell the properties back to his parent at cost price, meaning that Zorba's term of [[Prison/Legends|imprisonment]] could prove potentially lucrative for the old Hutt. Additionally, forty-five years was insignificant in the long lifetime of a Hutt; Zorba's imprisonment seemed more like a vacation for him.<ref name="HHH" /> While in prison, Zorba occasionally sent envoys to various clan meetings to represent him.<ref name="Rebel Dawn" /> Jabba performed even better than Zorba had anticipated, using various unscrupulous tactics to usurp Jiliac and become Desilijic leader. Zorba watched with glee, awaiting the day of his release, so that he could retire on Jabba's hard-earned funds.<ref name="HHH" /> At some point during his stay on Kip, Zorba briefly conversed with an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]]; the two were at odds, though, as the Grand Moff did not share Zorba's high opinion of Hutts and their culture.<ref name="ZtHR" />

In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Jabba Desilijic Tiure was [[Battle of the Great Pit of Carkoon|killed]] by [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], a prominent member of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. The Desilijic clan was left in disarray, with many people laying claims to his various organizations; eventually, after months of infighting, two Hutts named [[Kumac]] and [[Jelasi]] ascended to the position once held by Zorba.<ref name="NEGTC" /> As Kip was such a backwater world, news of this never reached Zorba. However, [[5 ABY/Legends|less than a year]] after Jabba's demise, Kip was conquered by [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], and the prisoners of the Girigard Subsurface Correctional Facility were all freed in the chaos. Kip's natives had been unable to operate the ''Zorba Express'', so Zorba found it exactly where it was left before his imprisonment; the old Hutt dug up his supply of gemstones and set a course for his son's residence on Tatooine.<ref name="ZtHR" /> Unbeknownst to Zorba, his release had been engineered by [[Ysanne Isard]], the [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]] and ruler of the Empire, who hoped to use Zorba for her own clandestine purposes.<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

===Zorba the Hutt's revenge===
{{Dialogue|Zorba|I am Zorba the Hutt, the father of Jabba! I want someone to tell me where I can find my son! I was told that Hutts are no longer permitted in Jabba's palace! Who owns the palace, if not Jabba?|Tibor|If I were you, Zorba, I'd calm down. Have yourself a zoochberry juice.|Zorba|I will ''not'' calm down! I want information about Jabba! And I'll pay five gemstones to anyone who talks!|Hissa|You seem to be about the only creature this side of the Dune Sea who doesn't know that Jabba the Hutt is dead.|attr=Zorba, Tibor, and Bertroff Hissa|src=<ref name="ZtHR" />}}
Zorba arrived at Jabba's Palace on Tatooine, but was not admitted by its [[TT-8L gatekeeper droid/Legends|TT-8L gatekeeper droid]], who informed him that the palace was under new ownership. When the gatekeeper [[Droid/Legends|droid]] learned he was a Hutt, it retracted, leaving him with no option but to leave. Confused and furious, Zorba made his way to the [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley Spaceport]], hoping to find answers about Jabba's whereabouts in [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina]]. The [[Cantina/Legends|cantina's]] denizens—among them Grand Moff [[Bertroff Hissa]], who was on official Imperial business, and [[Tibor]], a notorious [[Barabel/Legends|Barabel]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]]—told Zorba the bad news: Jabba had been strangled to [[Death/Legends|death]] by his slave, Leia Organa, at [[Great Pit of Carkoon/Legends|Carkoon]]. Distraught, Zorba swore he would get his revenge on Organa, and hired a group of bounty hunters, led by Tibor, to recapture the dormant Jabba's Palace.<ref name="ZtHR" />

Zorba quickly recovered from the shock, and began planning how he could use the situation to his advantage.<ref name="HHH" /> No will of Jabba's had been located, so many of his more lucrative properties and operations had been taken by local authorities; Zorba, however, knew of Jabba's secret "true will and testament," a [[Hologram/Legends|holorecording]] kept in the memory banks of a hidden droid named [[CB-99]]. Zorba and his entourage of bounty hunters located the droid and recovered the will; in the recording Jabba stated that he wished his entire legacy to go to Zorba, including his desert palace, his [[Desilijic Complex/Legends|townhouse]] in Mos Eisley, and the [[Holiday Towers Hotel and Casino|Holiday Towers]] at [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]], [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. While Zorba claimed the will was authentic,<ref name="ZtHR" /> many suspected it was a forgery.<ref name="HHH" />

In the ''Zorba Express'', the old Hutt and Tibor made their way to Bespin to reclaim Jabba's properties on Cloud City; it was also the last known location of Leia Organa.<ref name="ZtHR" /> En route, Zorba spread the word among various criminal empires about Jabba's will.<ref name="HHH" /> The Holiday Towers Hotel and Casino had been taken over by [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], the [[Baron Administrator/Legends|Baron Administrator]] of Cloud City, and, despite his demands, Zorba was not admitted entry into Jabba's former penthouse suite. Zorba requested an audience with Calrissian; while he waited for the baron-administrator to arrive, he sampled the [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] tables offered by the [[Casino/Legends|casino]], though rarely won any games. When Calrissian finally arrived, Zorba again voiced his demands for the hotel to be handed over. Calrissian refused, though, and knowing Zorba would never relent, he offered to challenge the Hutt in a game of sabacc. If Calrissian won the game, Zorba would leave the [[City/Legends|city]] and never return; if Zorba won, Calrissian would hand over Cloud City and appoint him [[Governor/Legends|governor]]. Zorba insisted that as they were in the property willed to him by Jabba, they use his deck of cards—which was marked with [[Ultraviolet/Legends|ultraviolet]] light, visible to Hutts but not [[Human/Legends|Humans]].<ref name="ZtHR" />

[[File:ZorbaTricoulus.jpg|thumb|250px|Trioculus and Zorba, who both wanted Leia Organa for very different reasons.]]
Using the marked deck, Zorba triumphed over Calrissian, winning control of Cloud City. Zorba banished Calrissian and immediately took over the city. He hoped to reestablish the tourist trade that had once thrived on Cloud City, but had since fallen on hard times. The main obstacle for this was the "[[braze]]" created by a nearby factory barge under the control of would-be [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Trioculus]], and Zorba made it his priority to negotiate with Trioculus to attempt to cut back the factory's toxic emissions. Meanwhile, Tibor had learned that Leia Organa was on Bespin, in the captivity of Trioculus; he told Zorba that Trioculus might trade her for [[Ken]], a so-called [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] wanted by Trioculus who had been apprehended by the [[Bespin Wing Guard/Legends|Cloud Police]]. To ensure that Ken was actually the "Jedi Prince" Trioculus coveted, Zorba decided to question him; Ken was initially defiant, refusing to cooperate, but a dosage of [[avabush spice]]—a potent truth serum—and some careful questions from Zorba eventually persuaded him to reveal that he was, in fact, the much sought-after Jedi Prince.<ref name="ZtHR" />

Zorba arranged a meeting with Trioculus, and allowed the former [[Supreme Slavelord|slavelord]] to himself interrogate Ken. Zorba made his offer, pledging to hand over Ken if Trioculus closed down his factory and presented Organa to him. Trioculus, who had plans to make Organa his wife, vehemently refused, despite Zorba's protestations, and Zorba pronounced the two mortal enemies. Each of them signaled for their security forces to enter the meeting room, but Zorba's Cloud Police were far more numerous than Trioculus's [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]; Zorba took the slavelord captive, and threatened to encase him in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]]. With Trioculus imprisoned, Zorba led a ragtag [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of bounty hunter vessels in an [[Skirmish on Bespin|attack]] on the Imperial factory barge, where Organa was being kept. The ships made light work of the barge, destroying it within several minutes. Satisfied that Organa was dead and Jabba was avenged, Zorba returned to Cloud City to mock Trioculus. Trioculus vowed that an armada of Imperial ships would slag Zorba's city, but Zorba indeed encased Trioculus in carbonite and kept him in Cloud City, so that the Imperials would not attack.<ref name="ZtHR" />

===In the belly of the beast===
{{Quote|A-haw-haw-haw…! So, Princess Leia, at last we meet. Allow me to introduce myself. I am Zorba, Jabba the Hutt's father. And nobody is better at getting even with a murdering Princess like you than a clever old Hutt like me!|Zorba, to Leia Organa|<ref name="QotE">''[[Queen of the Empire]]''</ref>}}
Zorba had a mimicry of Trioculus's carbonite slab placed in the [[Figg & Associates Art Museum]], while he kept the real one in his ship.<ref name="QotE" /> During his time administering Cloud City, Zorba began formulating a plan to turn Jabba's dormant palace into a high-security prison, where myriad prisoners could be kept. Shortly thereafter, members of the [[Central Committee of Grand Moffs]] rescued a counterfeit slab of carbonite that they believed was Trioculus's body.<ref name="MfMY">''[[Mission from Mount Yoda]]''</ref> Zorba's efforts into making Cloud City a profitable business venture were being met with little success—despite destroying the factory barge and reducing the amount of braze, Zorba had lost over eighteen million [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] in revenue; his audit droids informed him that this was because most tourists were choosing to visit [[Hologram Fun World]] instead of casinos. Zorba roused his bounty hunter team and made his way to Hologram Fun World in the ''Zorba Express'', hoping to scare its visitors away.<ref name="QotE" />

[[File:ZorbaThrownIntoTheSarlacc-QueenOfTheEmpire.jpg|thumb|left|190px|Trioculus has Zorba thrown into the [[Sarlacc/Legends|sarlacc]].]]
Zorba and his cohorts terrorized the tourists in Hologram Fun World, looting, plundering, and vandalizing. After a time, hordes of visitors fled, and Tibor had stolen a large amount of precious stones. During his vandalism spree, the Barabel bounty hunter had noticed Leia Organa and her fiancé, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], entering a performance of the renowned magician [[Bithabus]] the Mystifier—she had survived the destruction of the factory barge on Bespin. Tibor informed Zorba, but the Hutt quickly overcame his surprise and hatched a plan. One of his gang of bounty hunters, [[Cobak]], was the [[Bith/Legends|same species]] as Bithabus; Zorba's minions captured the magician and gave his costume to Cobak, who would be able to pass for Bithabus. Cobak was able to lure Organa onto the stage and into a cage, which was taken by Tibor to the ''Zorba Express''.<ref name="QotE" />

Not wanting Organa to escape death again, Zorba and Tibor decided to take her to Carkoon on Tatooine, where she had killed Jabba, and feed her to the [[Sarlacc/Legends|sarlacc]] pit. He spoke with her for the first time, though Organa was defiant, claiming that she had killed Jabba in self defense; Zorba had her locked up alongside Trioculus's body for the entirety of the journey to Tatooine.<ref name="QotE" />

As the ''Zorba Express'' approached Tatooine, it was attacked by an Imperial vessel—the Central Committee of Grand Moffs's [[Moffship]]—which used a [[Tractor/Legends|tractor]] beam to halt Zorba's progress. The Hutt armed himself with a portable [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]], while Tibor used dual [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]], as the Imperials began boarding. Zorba and Tibor killed many of the attacking stormtroopers, but Grand Moff Hissa fired several smoke bombs, which impeded Zorba and Tibor's efforts. Zorba was captured and chained, and the Moffs located and thawed Trioculus, who threatened to kill Zorba; the Hutt was unperturbed, though, knowing that he had a trump card in Leia Organa. Zorba negotiated his release in exchange for informing the Moffs of Organa's location in his ship. After locating her, Trioculus broke his promise—he dropped Zorba from the Moffship into the sarlacc pit. The immense beast swallowed the Hutt whole—but later, the combination of the Hutt's bulk and protective mucus coating gave the sarlacc a fit of [[indigestion]]. The sarlacc vomited Zorba into the desert night.<ref name="QotE" />

===Later life===
{{Quote|Once I get to Mos Eisley, I'll find a fearless cargo pilot willing to take me deep into the Null Zone, all the way to Space Station Scardia to see Kadann and his Prophets of the Dark Side. Just wait until the grand moffs see what I have in store for them. A-haw-haw-haw-haw-hawwwww…!|Zorba the Hutt, talking to himself in the Tatooinian desert|<ref name="PotDS">[[Prophets of the Dark Side (novel)|''Prophet of the Dark Side'']]</ref>}}
Zorba roamed the desert without food or [[Water/Legends|water]] for days, waiting for someone to arrive and help him escape. Eventually, a [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] [[Sandcrawler/Legends|sandcrawler]] gave Zorba passage to Mos Eisley, in return for a hundred slightly used [[Spin-and-Win machine]]s from the Holiday Towers casino. In Mos Eisley, Zorba hired a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] to take him to [[Scardia Station|Space Station Scardia]] in the [[Null Zone/Legends|Null Zone]]—the residence of the false [[Prophets of the Dark Side]], Trioculus and the Committee's enemies. Zorba hoped to sell the Prophets what little information he knew of Trioculus and the Committee's plans, so that he could get his revenge on those who fed him to the sarlacc. Zorba was able to hire a cargo [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] to deliver him to the Prophets, who, upon hearing he had information about Trioculus, welcomed him like an honored guest, with a banquet.<ref name="PotDS" />

Zorba had little to tell the leader of the Prophets, [[Rajah Ubooki|Kadann]], though he informed the false Prophet that Trioculus and his allies sought to find the [[Lost City of the Jedi]], and that the Moffs were traitors. While Zorba's information was of little use to the Prophets, he was able to provide a testimony—via recording—in the illegitimate trial of the Grand Moffs, conducted by the Prophets aboard Space Station Scardia. Zorba claimed they were plotting against Kadann, and that they should be harshly punished; he also stated that the Committee's leader, Bertroff Hissa, should receive a harsher sentence for worse crimes. Ultimately, Hissa perished on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]],<ref name="PotDS" /> Kadann was killed by [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Afsheen Makati]],<ref name="who">{{InsiderCite|66|Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals}}</ref> and the remaining Moffs were wiped out by Ysanne Isard.<ref name="NEC" />

[[File:Zorba_the_Brat.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Zorba and his loyal henchman, Tibor.]]
Zorba left Space Station Scardia, and with his plot to kill Organa foiled, he returned to Hutt Space.<ref name="HHH" /> At some point, Zorba ceded ownership of Cloud City back to Calrissian, who gave it to the [[Ugnaught/Legends|Ugnaught]] workers who inhabited it.<ref name="NEGTC" /> Zorba's other children carried on where he had left off in the hunt for Organa and Solo, posting a huge bounty;<ref name="DEE" /> again, it was not met with success, and around [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]] it was rescinded. Through the Hutt courts, Zorba used Jabba's will to lay claim to his son's criminal empire, and control of the Desilijic clan was wrested from the hands of Kumac and Jelasi. Since Jabba's death, the Besadii clan—led by [[Durga Besadii Tai]]—had overtaken Desilijic as the most successful of the Hutt clans, and Zorba could never build Desilijic back into anything remotely like what his son had done.<ref name="NEGTC" />

Zorba's final reign as a crime lord was short-lived, however. Some time after Zorba took control of the clan, the Hutt Grand Council deemed Jabba's "true will and testament" to be a forgery, and Zorba was disgraced. His properties and clan leadership were taken from him by a multitude of other relatives, and Zorba was left virtually penniless. Since the will was declared false, Zorba became extremely reclusive and was not seen in public for some time. Rumors abounded about his death, and they were considered to be accurate.<ref name="HHH" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|That slimy, foul, disgusting, ugly, odorous, slobbering, dirty rotten slug!|Han Solo|<ref name="QotE" />}}
[[File:ZorbaFace-QE.png|thumb|left|250px|Zorba Desilijic Tiure.]]
Zorba Desilijic Tiure was extremely proud of his Hutt heritage. He believed Hutts to be a fair and honorable [[Species/Legends|species]]; they were kind to each other, even if they expected everyone else to do their bidding. He often swore by the name of the ancient conqueror, [[Kossak Inijic Ar'durv|Kossak the Hutt]], and it pained him to hear other [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] speak ill of Hutts. Zorba was outraged when the gatekeeper droid at Jabba's Palace refused to admit him, believing it was because of anti-Hutt bias.<ref name="ZtHR" /> Zorba was also extremely close to his family, especially Jabba,<ref name="HHH" /><ref name="ZtHR" /> though Jabba and Jiliac did not have high opinions of Zorba.<ref name="Rebel Dawn" />

Zorba was unlike most Hutts in that he did not relish being in charge of criminal operations, and he often shunned power—for the almost 600 years he spent as leader of Desilijic clan, he was never really interested in running the clan and the family business.<ref name="HHH" /> Despite this, he was an iron-fisted ruler, and he did not allow any attempts to usurp him to succeed.<ref name="NEGTC" /> Zorba was something of an entrepreneur, and he was a Hutt full of ideas.<ref name="Rebel Dawn" /><ref name="MfMY" /><ref name="ZtHR" /> He was constantly looking to see how he could expand his criminal empire in less-than-orthodox ways; he wanted to turn Jabba's Palace into a maximum security prison,<ref name="MfMY" /> and attempted to make Bespin a popular tourist destination.<ref name="ZtHR" /><ref name="QotE" /> Zorba was totally inept at managing finances and running his criminal empire,<ref name="HHH" /> and he almost bankrupted Desilijic with his outlandish schemes.<ref name="Rebel Dawn" />

Zorba was almost universally disliked by his fellow Hutts,<ref name="HHH" /><ref name="Rebel Dawn" /> and by most non-Hutts.<ref name="ZtHR" /><ref name="QotE" /> Because of the rare genetic throwback that gave him a full head of hair, Zorba was often ostracized by other Hutts—they felt he was impure, and unfit to be in such a lofty position, and made no attempts to hide their displeasure at it.<ref name="HHH" /> Zorba was a vengeful being, and he had no qualms about harming innocent people to achieve his own means. On Bespin, he destroyed an Imperial factory barge, killing all of its workers, in an attempt to kill Leia Organa,<ref name="ZtHR" /> and he and his cohorts terrorized visitors at Hologram Fun World in order to alternatively attract them to his casinos in Cloud City.<ref name="QotE" />

==Behind the scenes==
Zorba the Hutt was created by [[Paul Davids|Paul]] and [[Hollace Davids]] for the [[Adventures of a Jedi Prince|''Jedi Prince'']] series of young reader's books. He first appeared in the third book, ''[[Zorba the Hutt's Revenge]]'', in which he served as the main antagonist, and he made fleeting appearances in the next three books in the series, ''[[Mission from Mount Yoda]]'', ''[[Queen of the Empire]]'', and [[Prophets of the Dark Side (novel)|''Prophets of the Dark Side'']]. While the series is generally marginalized by the rest of the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]], Zorba has been prevalent in other works. [[Tom Veitch]]'s [[Dark Empire endnotes|''Dark Empire'' endnotes]] were the first source to mention him, in the same year that ''Zorba the Hutt's Revenge'' was released, and he has since been referenced in [[A. C. Crispin]]'s ''[[Rebel Dawn]]''; numerous [[Star Wars: Essential Guides|Essential Guides]]; ''[[Episode I Adventures 7: Capture Arawynne]]'', by [[Dave Wolverton]]; [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]], by [[Paul Ens]] and [[Pablo Hidalgo]]; a [[Wizards of the Coast]] online article called ''[[Hutt! Hutt! Hutt!]]'', which deals with his early life; and in [[Karen Traviss]]' [[Clone Wars (disambiguation)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'']] [[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (novelization)|novelization]].
